message:
fix(planner): reachable expression-index usability + negative-literal WHERE support

Two correctness fixes in fsqlite-planner that both trace back to predicates
the planner silently threw away.

1. analyze_index_usability (#63).  Expression indexes store their real key
   terms in `expression_columns` and leave `columns` empty by convention,
   but the guard order was:

       if index.columns.is_empty() { return NotUsable }
       if !index.expression_columns.is_empty() { ... expression path ... }

   so every expression index bailed out before reaching the expression path
   and WHERE clauses like `lower(name) = 'alice'` degraded to a full table
   scan even when a matching `CREATE INDEX ... ON users(lower(name), id)`
   was in the schema.  Reorder: expression-index check runs first, then
   the empty-column guard, then the plain-column path.

2. analyze_expression_index_usability.  Once the expression path is
   reachable, it still has to handle the fact that classify_where_term
   only stamps a term as `WhereTermKind::Equality` when the LHS of `=` is
   a bare column — `lower(name) = 'alice'` has a FunctionCall on the LHS,
   so the term is classified as `Other` even though it is structurally
   `<expr> = <literal>`.  Walk the raw BinaryOp/Between AST directly and
   match structurally against `expression_columns[0]` (both orientations,
   skipping NULL equality per SQL semantics).  Added a two-pass walk so
   Equality beats Range when both are present on the same key expression,
   matching the plain-column behaviour.

Regression coverage in crates/fsqlite-planner/src/lib.rs:
  - test_index_usability_expression_index_equality — the bounded repro
    from the issue (`lower(name) = 'alice'`) must reach Equality.
  - test_index_usability_expression_index_non_matching — a WHERE on
    `upper(name)` against an index on `lower(name)` must stay NotUsable.
  - test_index_usability_empty_index_still_not_usable — a degenerate
    index with neither `columns` nor `expression_columns` must still
    fall through to NotUsable (guards the reorder from widening).

Closes #63.

--- differential negative-literal filter ---

While running the full planner test suite I also uncovered a pre-existing
failure in the differential-view proptests:

    test differential_rowset_generator_compiles_to_expected_plan ... FAILED
    `SELECT a FROM a WHERE a = -1` fails with "differential WHERE clauses
    only support column = literal predicates joined by AND"

The SQL parser correctly represents `-1` as `UnaryOp(Negate, Literal(1))`,
but differential::match_literal_equality only accepted bare
`Expr::Literal`, so every negative integer/float literal in a filter
dropped the entire query onto the unsupported path.  New helper
`extract_literal_value` walks through UnaryOp::{Plus,Negate} wrappers
and returns an `Option<SqliteValue>` directly (with wrapping-safe
integer negation), letting the filter compile correctly.

Also widened `arb_identifier` to exclude the rest of the SQL keyword set
(in, to, or, not, is, null, into, case, when, then, else, end, union,
intersect, except, with, values, having, offset, between, like, glob,
match, regexp, cross, natural, outer, right, full, set, exists, of,
isnull, notnull).  Without those filters the generator would periodically
hand the parser a keyword as an identifier and the proptest would fail
through no fault of the compiler under test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
